mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines over consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE breaches 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a spike in core inflation indicates stagflationary pressure, which forces restrictive policy while corporate margins compress, yielding negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% MoM
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not systematically negative when GDPC1 contracts alongside rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy demand absorption, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns fail to correlate positively with INDPRO expansions during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a sharp deceleration in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the following month as growth expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not decline significantly when HOUST drops below 1.1 million units.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls surge above 250,000 while real consumer spending simultaneously contracts, it indicates a labor market disconnect from actual demand, forecasting margin compression and negative equity returns over a week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not negatively correlated with divergent PAYEMS and RSAFS movements.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP above the 2.0% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which historically supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not exhibit positive skew when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as real yields spike.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not decline significantly following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM.
